operating_state: Worldwide revenue increased 5.8% to $23.7 billion driven by U.S. growth and international expansion. Innovative Medicine grew 4.9% with oncology achieving 24.0% growth led by DARZALEX and CARVYKTI expansion, offset by immunology declining 15.4% from STELARA biosimilar competition. MedTech expanded 7.3% led by cardiovascular devices including SHOCKWAVE acquisition contribution, while orthopaedics declined due to restructuring disruption. Neuroscience grew 15.1% with CAPLYTA contributing following Intra-Cellular acquisition.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 67.9% from 69.4% reflecting unfavorable product mix from STELARA decline and increased intangible asset amortization from recent acquisitions. Selling and administrative expenses declined as percentage of sales to 24.8% from corporate rationalization, while research and development remained stable at 14.8%. Operating expenses included acquisition-related costs and elevated intangible amortization.
material_events: Completed Intra-Cellular Therapies acquisition for CAPLYTA franchise funded through senior unsecured notes issuance. Reversed talc litigation reserves following Texas Bankruptcy Court dismissal and finalized global settlement agreements. Initiated Surgery franchise restructuring program while continuing Orthopaedics restructuring. Paid quarterly dividend of $1.30 per share representing 4.8% increase marking 63rd consecutive year of increases.
macro_exposures: STELARA faces continued biosimilar competition with launches expected throughout 2025 following patent settlements. Foreign currency translation resulted in negative impact with international operations facing headwinds. Medicare Part D redesign shifts greater cost burden to manufacturers while China volume-based procurement affects Surgery and Orthopaedics franchises. Commercial paper borrowings increased with weighted average interest rate of 4.28%.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued oncology portfolio growth driven by DARZALEX and newer launches with Intra-Cellular integration contributing to neuroscience trajectory. Surgery franchise restructuring expected to complete over two years while Orthopaedics restructuring substantially complete by fiscal 2025 end. Company maintains focus on external innovation through strategic acquisitions while pursuing comprehensive talc litigation resolution.
uncertainty_profile: Pending tax legislation changes including GILTI rate increase expected to result in one-time deferred tax remeasurement cost with ongoing audits maintaining unrecognized tax benefit liabilities. Talc litigation exposure remains significant with pending plaintiffs following bankruptcy dismissal. Multiple patent challenges threaten key product exclusivity with ongoing litigation across major revenue contributors. Government investigations continue including DOJ demands and False Claims Act cases.
